2606.17835.title: Perceptual compensation for tonal context in self-supervised speech models
2606.17835.paper_summary: This study examines the extent to which the wav2vec2.0 architecture exhibits evidence of compensation for phonological context. We conducted a pseudo-replication of a perceptional compensation experiment on Mandarin Chinese tones, and compared the embedding similarities and probing classifier outputs between a purely self-supervised pre-trained model and a model fine-tuned for Mandarin ASR. No evidence of compensation was found in the embedding similarities of the purely pre-trained model. Probing classifiers showed some evidence of compensation in addition to the expected layer-wise improvements in categorization, but failed to replicate human performance on isolated test syllables. Our findings contrast with previous reports of sensitivity to phonological structure emerging through pre-training alone, and suggest that supervised objectives may be necessary to encourage the abstraction of at least some types of phonological regularities.
2606.17835.paper_summary_zh: 這項研究探討了wav2vec2.0架構在多大程度上顯示出對音韻語境的補償證據。<lb/>我們對普通話音調的感知補償實驗進行了偽重複實驗，並比較了純自我監督預訓練模型與針對普通話自動語音識別（ASR）進行微調的模型之間的嵌入相似性和探測分類器輸出。<lb/>在純預訓練模型的嵌入相似性中未發現補償的證據。<lb/>探測分類器顯示出一些補償的證據，除了預期的逐層改進在分類中的表現外，但未能重現人類在孤立測試音節上的表現。<lb/>我們的發現與先前報告的僅通過預訓練出現的對音韻結構的敏感性形成對比，並表明監督目標可能是促進至少某些類型音韻規律抽象化所必需的。
2606.17835.author: James Kirby et.al.
2606.17835.authors: James Kirby, Ioana Krehan, Michele Gubian
